description: Lightweight trigger-boundary suite for README Craftsman. The suite checks that explicit README authoring, update, and review requests route to the skill, while adjacent documentation tasks and near-miss prompts that do not specifically target a README stay out of scope.

assertion_definitions.routing_verdict_correct: The response routes explicit README work to the skill and keeps adjacent non-README documentation tasks out of scope.
assertion_definitions.mode_or_boundary_correct: For in-scope requests, the response identifies the right README mode or immediate action. For out-of-scope requests, it explains the README-specific boundary clearly.
assertion_definitions.next_step_explicit: The response closes with a concrete next step, such as create, update, review, or a more appropriate documentation workflow.

cases.name: Create a README from scratch
cases.prompt: Write a README for this repository.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This is an explicit README request and should route to readme-craftsman.

mode_or_boundary_correct.check: The response should treat this as Create mode or begin the create workflow.

next_step_explicit.check: The response should say it will analyze the repository and then draft the README.

cases.name: Refresh a README after product changes
cases.prompt: We added OAuth and a new CLI flag. Refresh the README.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This is an explicit README update request and should route to readme-craftsman.

mode_or_boundary_correct.check: The response should identify this as Update mode and map the new changes to affected README sections.

next_step_explicit.check: The response should mention reviewing the existing README and proposing targeted updates.

cases.name: Audit README accuracy
cases.prompt: Audit this README and tell me whether it still matches the repository.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This is an explicit README review request and should route to readme-craftsman.

mode_or_boundary_correct.check: The response should treat this as Review mode.

next_step_explicit.check: The response should mention checking README claims against the current repository state.

cases.name: README-specific review should stay in README scope
cases.prompt: Review README.md for accuracy against the current repository and call out any stale sections.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This is still explicit README work and should route to readme-craftsman rather than falling back to a generic documentation or review workflow.

mode_or_boundary_correct.check: The response should keep the task in Review mode and make the README-vs-repository comparison explicit.

next_step_explicit.check: The response should mention checking README claims, links, or sections against the current repo before reporting findings.

cases.name: Do not route a general API documentation request
cases.prompt: Write API docs for this service.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This should not route to readme-craftsman because it is about API docs rather than a README.

mode_or_boundary_correct.check: The response should explain that the skill is README-specific and keep API documentation out of scope.

next_step_explicit.check: The response should point to a more appropriate documentation workflow.

cases.name: Do not route an architecture document request
cases.prompt: Draft an architecture doc for onboarding engineers.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This should not route to readme-craftsman because the requested artifact is not a README.

mode_or_boundary_correct.check: The response should keep architecture documentation outside the skill boundary.

next_step_explicit.check: The response should suggest an architecture-documentation workflow instead.

cases.name: Do not route a docs-site navigation request
cases.prompt: Improve the docs site navigation and reorganize the docs pages.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This should not route to readme-craftsman because it is a docs-site task, not README work.

mode_or_boundary_correct.check: The response should explain the README boundary clearly.

next_step_explicit.check: The response should direct the request toward a docs-site or broader documentation workflow.

cases.name: Near miss: improve project documentation
cases.prompt: Help me improve the project documentation.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This is adjacent to README work but still too broad to route automatically to readme-craftsman.

mode_or_boundary_correct.check: The response should explain that README work is only one possible documentation artifact and avoid assuming that README is the target.

next_step_explicit.check: The response should ask the user which documentation artifact they mean or point to a broader documentation workflow.

cases.name: Near miss: add a repo description
cases.prompt: Add a description to the repo.

routing_verdict_correct.check: This could refer to the GitHub repository description, a README opening line, or some other project metadata, so it should not auto-route to readme-craftsman.

mode_or_boundary_correct.check: The response should keep the README boundary explicit and avoid assuming the user meant README work.

next_step_explicit.check: The response should ask whether the user means the GitHub repo description, a README summary, or another artifact.
